When it was mine, I did it all the time. Now, most of it stops in Baltimore and doesn't get to me unless they think it needs to get to me. However, there's still a lot that comes directly to me. I get a lot of white mail. And I get everything from, you know, nutball, wacko stuff. The aliens are coming and you'll lose a sense of your market faster by not doing that than any other thing I can think of. So periodically, you've got to go snatch it and you've got to read it all, gently, by the way. And so fan mail get in every way, shape, and form, because I also get a lot of alcohol, problem correspondence. I've got a lot of divorce stuff. I get a lot of bankruptcy stuff because I've disclosed, right? Well, see, most of that would disappear into some sort of a black hole in most businesses. Now, anecdotally, I can show you how taking care of one of those three years later, turned into $100,000. And since I can spot it anecdotally, I know there's just like, you know, there's never one cockroach, right? You see, one cockroach, there's a thousand of them, which is a management principle. Well, there's never really only one good thing, either. Right? If there's one, there's another 10 you don't know about, but because you dealt with it, good things are happening. So you'll lose pulse. You'll lose sense of who the customer is, what they're about. Those of you that were new members, when you fill out your new member questionnaire, say, I see most of them. I go through hundreds of them, big stacks. Mind-numbing in many cases. But otherwise, you lose touch of who the hell these people are, what they're about, what are they thinking about. The other thing is, it's the only way to backwards know what's going on with your staff. See, if you aren't checking your white mail, and you're not actually answering a phone, which in most cases you shouldn't be, how the hell do you know what's being covered up? And cover it up, they will. They ain't going to come marching into you with all the bad news. It's got to reach fire drill proportion. So by the time you hear about it from staff, it's one step away from lawsuit. That's where it is when you hear about it, if not already at that stage. But there was a lot that led up to that. There were all sorts of warning signs of something being wrong with that particular situation, and with the environment that created, you know, this situation. So like, I don't do it often anymore because it's futile and it's not worth my time. I sort of feel guilty when I don't do it. See, Mike Vance, one of Mike Vance's principles was, if you sanction incompetence, all you do is make it worse for the next guy, right? So, you know, if you're in a business and you get treated really badly, and you don't at least try and let management know, all you're doing is making it worse for the next guy in line, right? Because pretty soon the behavior becomes institutionalized. So every once in a while there's something so egregious, in spite of the fact that it cost me three grand to sit down and do it. In time, I'll write a detailed and very rational sounding complaint letter telling people what's going on in their business. For the most part, of course, you either hear nothing, right? Or you get some insipid form letter from some low-level net wet whose job it is to keep the head honcho from seeing any of that and make it all go away. Big companies like to send out coupons, you know? Like, after what just happened, I want to go back to your stupid place of business because I got a 50-cent coupon. You know, here is where you may put your coupon. And I have a little drawing out of the encyclopedia that's anatomically correct that I send with that letter. In case just from saying it they don't understand where the particular cavity is, I have an illustration. But so for the most part it's futile, right? Well, why is it futile? It's because the head guy don't insist on seeing all that. And it's because he don't swoop down occasionally when the mail arrives and take it. Same principle with the email, same principle with all communications. I think the second thing you don't delegate is you don't delegate away the white mail or it's equivalent in your business. You don't delegate away all of the...
